_comment: This file is OVERWRITTEN each security review. Do NOT append to it.

ssh_attacks.fail2ban_status: active - sshd jail running, 14 currently banned, 7734 total banned

pdf_security.document_write_safe: 1 usage in keyboard-shortcuts.js line 416 — writes to newly opened blank window for print cheat sheet only. All content escaped via escapeHtml(). Acceptable pattern.
pdf_security.innerHTML_note: 247 usages across 57 JS files (unchanged — guides.js uses 0 innerHTML). All PDF-derived content escaped. markdown-export.js simpleMarkdownToHtml escapes &<> but not double quotes — CSP-mitigated.

guides_js.canvas_rendering: All visual output via Canvas 2D API (fillRect, fillText, strokeRect) — no HTML injection surface
guides_js.localStorage_usage: JSON.parse(stored) in try/catch with fallback to defaults. Same-origin only. LOW risk.
guides_js.grid_loops_bounded: Minor grid loops guarded by spacingPx >= 4 (line 146/168). Major grid loops use majorSpacingPx = spacingPx * 4. Canvas dimensions provide natural upper bound.
guides_js.drawRuler_loop: MEDIUM: line 685 — infinite-form for loop with break at pos > canvas size. If tickPx = 0 (zoom = 0), loop never terminates. Should add guard: if (tickPx < 1) return;
guides_js.grid_spacing_clamped: Input clamped to [4, 144] at line 840 via Math.max/Math.min. Good.
guides_js.no_user_content_in_dom: Module renders only grid lines, rulers, and snap guides. No PDF content or user text inserted into DOM.

task076_integration_review.clipboard_js: Guide integration at lines 10, 794, 850-852, 863. Imports notifyDragStart/snapPosition/notifyDragEnd. Only passes numeric coords and internal IDs. No new security concerns.
task076_integration_review.signatures_js: Guide integration at lines 10, 714-716, 727. Imports notifyDragStart/snapPosition/notifyDragEnd. Only passes numeric coords. No new security concerns.
task076_integration_review.stamps_js: Guide integration at lines 8, 382, 390-392, 400, 423, 430, 438. Imports notifyDragStart/snapPosition/snapResize/notifyDragEnd. Only passes numeric coords. No new security concerns.
task076_integration_review.text_overlay_js: Guide integration at lines 8, 580-582, 591, 629, 639. Imports notifyDragStart/snapPosition/snapResize/notifyDragEnd. Only passes numeric coords. No new security concerns.

known_issues: server_tokens set to 'build' - leaks nginx version (nginx/1.26.3 Ubuntu) in Server header. Should be 'off'.
No general *.md nginx block - only CLAUDE.md specifically blocked. Any new .md file added to webroot would be publicly accessible.
UFW firewall is inactive - only SSH and HTTP/HTTPS ports are needed but no firewall enforces this.
disable_symlinks off in nginx config - low risk since only novakj can write to webroot.
CGI location block exists in nginx but no CGI scripts present - dead config that could be removed.
Codex proxy at /codex returns 502 - backend not running. No security risk but dead endpoint.

merge.js, batch.js, hyperlinks.js, bates.js, command-palette.js, keyboard-shortcuts.js, form-data.js, link-manager.js, reading-progress.js, bookmark-editor.js, layers.js use escapeHtml()/escHtml() that does not escape double quotes. CSP script-src 'self' mitigates inline handler injection. In most files used in element content (safe). In attribute contexts (title/aria-label/value/data-col) should add escapeAttr().

layers.js lines 505-507: OCG usage dictionary values (printState/viewState/exportState) from PDF inserted into innerHTML without escaping. A crafted PDF could inject HTML in the layer details popover. CSP blocks script execution. MEDIUM-LOW risk.
layers.js line 295,297,300: escapeHtml(group.name) in aria-label and title attributes — escapeHtml doesn't escape double quotes. Layer names from PDF OCG structure. CSP mitigates. LOW risk.
layers.js local downloadBlob() does not use sanitizeFilename from utils.js — download attribute prevents path traversal but inconsistent with other modules. LOW risk.
layers.js exportFlattenLayers: no processing timeout — renders all pages at 2x scale. Client-side self-DoS only for large PDFs. LOW risk.

bookmark-editor.js line 461: escapeHtml(item.title) in value attribute inside innerHTML template — escapeHtml doesn't escape double quotes. Bookmark titles from PDF outline or user input. CSP blocks inline handlers. Self-XSS only. LOW risk.
reading-progress.js line 387: bookmark name (bm.name) used unescaped in title attribute inside innerHTML. Self-XSS only — user sets own bookmark names, data in same-origin localStorage. LOW risk.
reading-progress.js line 550: escapeHtml(doc.name) in title attribute — escapeHtml doesn't escape double quotes. Self-XSS only — doc.name from user-selected file. LOW risk.

hyperlinks.js: detected PDF link URLs not validated for protocol scheme. A malicious PDF could contain javascript: URIs in link annotations. CSP + noopener/noreferrer mitigate. Should add protocol allowlist (http/https/mailto) before window.open().
hyperlinks.js line 686: email link window.open() missing noopener/noreferrer — minor since mailto: links are low risk.
img2pdf.js image validation uses OR logic (MIME type OR extension) - slightly permissive but acceptable for client-side processing.
insert-pages.js has dead code: escapeHtml() function defined but never called.
metadata.js has dead code: escapeHtml() function defined but never called.
batch.js line 294: escapeHtml() used with textContent causes double-escaping — cosmetic display bug for filenames containing &, <, >.
keyboard-shortcuts.js line 416: document.write used on new popup window for print cheat sheet — acceptable pattern but noted for tracking.
headers-footers.js: custom preset names from prompt() not length-limited before localStorage storage — minor, only affects local storage size.
form-data.js: template names from prompt() not length-limited before localStorage storage — minor, same pattern as headers-footers.js.
page-labels.js: custom preset names from prompt() not length-limited before localStorage storage — minor, same pattern as headers-footers.js and form-data.js.

Session restore (TASK-036): restoreNotes/restoreAnnotations/restoreDrawings/restoreSignatures/restoreFormValues do not validate data schema from IndexedDB. IndexedDB is same-origin so risk is self-XSS only. Low priority.
link-manager.js Find & Replace: new RegExp() from user input could cause ReDoS with pathological patterns. Low impact — only runs against URL strings (typically short). Errors caught.
compare.js escapeHtml() does not escape double quotes — same pattern as other modules. Only used in element content context in HTML report, not in attributes. Safe.

snip.js line 865: innerHTML with template literal uses internally-generated values (pageNum, format, timestamp) — not user-controlled. LOW risk but should ideally use textContent/DOM API for defense-in-depth.
snip.js: no escapeHtml utility defined — acceptable since no user-controlled strings are inserted via innerHTML, but would be good practice to add.
snip.js: selections array unbounded — no max limit. Low risk since user-driven interaction.
snip.js line 661: raw err.message from clipboard API shown in toast — minor info disclosure, clipboard errors are browser-generated.
image-manager.js: no file size limit on replacement images — client-side self-DoS only, low risk.

verify-signatures.js: ASN.1 recursive parser (parseASN1) has no recursion depth limit — deeply nested crafted DER structure could cause stack overflow. LOW-MEDIUM risk. Input bounded by PKCS#7 Contents field.
verify-signatures.js: no upper bound on signature count parsed from ByteRange regex. Thousands of fake entries could slow UI rendering. LOW risk.

verify-signatures.js: byteRange values from PDF not bounds-checked against file length. Uint8Array.slice() handles gracefully (returns truncated array) but could produce incorrect hash comparisons silently. LOW risk.
verify-signatures.js: ASN.1 DER length field not validated against remaining data — parser continues with truncated view. LOW risk.
verify-signatures.js: escapeHtml() does not escape double quotes — same pattern as other modules. Only used in element content. sig.status in class names is hardcoded, not PDF-derived. SAFE.
undo-redo.js: MEMORY_BUDGET_BYTES (50MB) defined but not enforced — only count-based trimming (MAX_HISTORY=100). Commands holding large page data could exceed memory budget. LOW risk for client-side.
undo-redo.js: escapeHtml() does not escape double quotes — same pattern as other modules. Only used in element content (cmd.description). SAFE.
page-resize.js: no processing timeout on applyResize() — client-side self-DoS only for very large PDFs. LOW risk.
bookmark-editor.js: outlineToTree() recursive with no explicit depth limit when loading PDF outlines. PDF outlines rarely exceed ~20 levels. LOW risk.
bookmark-editor.js: jsonToTree() recursive with no depth limit on imported JSON. User imports own files. LOW risk.
font-inspector.js: exportReportJSON() does not use sanitizeFilename from utils.js — download attribute prevents path traversal but inconsistent with other modules. LOW risk.
font-inspector.js: no explicit processing timeout on scanFonts() — has abortScan mechanism but no auto-timeout. Client-side self-DoS only. LOW risk.

markdown-export.js line 992: innerHTML = simpleMarkdownToHtml(content) — simpleMarkdownToHtml escapes &<> (line 1012) but not double quotes. Link URLs from PDF annotations reach <a href> at line 1029. Double-quote in URL could break attribute. javascript: URIs possible. CSP script-src 'self' blocks both vectors. MEDIUM-LOW risk.

markdown-export.js lines 942-945: YAML frontmatter — PDF metadata Title/Author embedded with double quotes but not escaped. Quotes/newlines in metadata break YAML syntax in exported .md file. Data integrity issue, no direct XSS. LOW risk.
markdown-export.js: no explicit processing timeout on buildDocument(). Yields every BATCH_SIZE (10) pages via setTimeout. Client-side self-DoS only. LOW risk.

bg-borders.js handleImageFile accepts image/svg+xml MIME type but applyBackgroundToPage only embeds PNG/JPEG via pdf-lib. SVG images render in canvas preview only (browser-sandboxed Image). SVG scripts/external refs blocked by browser when loaded as Image src. SAFE but slightly misleading UX — SVG backgrounds won't appear in final PDF output.
bg-borders.js no explicit processing timeout on applyBgBorders(). Yields every 5 pages. Client-side self-DoS only. LOW risk.
clipboard.js — no explicit blob size limit on clipboard images before processing. MAX_IMAGE_DIM=2000 downscaling mitigates. Client-side self-DoS only. LOW risk.
clipboard.js — pagePastes Map unbounded, no max paste item limit. Client-side self-DoS only. LOW risk.
clipboard.js — no processing timeout on processImageBlob() canvas conversion. LOW risk.

NEW: guides.js line 685 — drawRuler() infinite-form for loop. If tickPx = 0 (zoom = 0), loop never terminates and freezes UI. Should add guard: if (tickPx < 1) return; MEDIUM risk (client-side self-DoS).
NEW: guides.js — localStorage JSON.parse without schema validation. Same-origin only, falls back to defaults on error. LOW risk.
notes: Security review completed — no critical fixes needed. 1 new module (guides.js) reviewed + 4 modified files (TASK-076 integration). SECURE.

REVIEWED: guides.js — 1005 lines. Snap-to-grid and alignment guides module. Renders grid/rulers/snap guides via Canvas 2D API. 0 innerHTML usages. No eval, Function, document.write, fetch, window.open. Uses textContent for aria-live announcements. All visual output via canvas fillRect/fillText/strokeRect — no HTML injection surface. Grid loops bounded by spacingPx >= 4 guard. Input clamped [4,144]. SECURE.

REVIEWED: TASK-076 integration in clipboard.js, signatures.js, stamps.js, text-overlay.js — all 4 files import guide functions and pass only numeric coordinates and internal IDs. No new DOM manipulation or user-input handling. SECURE.
POSITIVE: guides.js uses Canvas 2D API exclusively for rendering — excellent security choice, eliminates HTML injection surface entirely.
JS file count: 75 (up from 74, +1 new module guides.js).
No new dependencies added since last review.
innerHTML count: 247 across 57 JS files (unchanged — guides.js uses 0 innerHTML).
SSH: fail2ban total bans 7734 (+101 since last review). 14 IPs currently banned (up from 8). Brute-force continues at typical rate. No successful unauthorized logins.
SSL certificate valid until June 18, 2026 (74 days remaining).
All security headers confirmed in HTTP response. Nginx blocking rules verified (git/env/CLAUDE.md/sh/traversal all return 404).
recommendations: MEDIUM: guides.js line 685 — add guard before drawRuler() tick loop: if (tickPx < 1) return; to prevent infinite loop when zoom approaches 0
MEDIUM-LOW: markdown-export.js line 1012 — add double-quote escaping to simpleMarkdownToHtml: text.replace(/"/g, '"') to prevent attribute breakout in <a href>
MEDIUM-LOW: markdown-export.js line 1029 — add protocol allowlist for link URLs: only allow http:, https:, mailto: schemes in generated <a href> tags
MEDIUM-LOW: markdown-export.js lines 942-945 — escape YAML special characters in frontmatter values: replace " with \" and newlines with spaces in title/author/filename
MEDIUM-LOW: Escape OCG usage values in layers.js lines 505-507 — wrap printState/viewState/exportState with escapeHtml() before inserting into HTML
MEDIUM-LOW: Add protocol allowlist to hyperlinks.js handleLinkClick() — only allow http:, https:, mailto: schemes before calling window.open() on detected PDF links
LOW: Add ASN.1 recursion depth limit (maxDepth=50) to verify-signatures.js parseASN1() for defense against crafted deeply nested structures
LOW: Add upper bound cap (MAX_SIGNATURES=100) to verify-signatures.js signature parsing loop
LOW: Add bounds validation on byteRange values in verify-signatures.js before slicing PDF bytes
LOW: Add escapeAttr() function to modules using escapeHtml() in attribute contexts (layers.js, bookmark-editor.js, reading-progress.js) — font-inspector.js already has this, use as reference
LOW: layers.js — use sanitizeFilename from utils.js in downloadBlob or import downloadBlob from utils.js for consistency
LOW: font-inspector.js — use sanitizeFilename from utils.js in exportReportJSON for consistency
LOW: Fix batch.js line 294 — use fileName directly with textContent instead of escapeHtml(fileName)
LOW: Set server_tokens off in nginx.conf to stop leaking version info (currently exposes nginx/1.26.3)
LOW: Enable UFW firewall (sudo ufw allow 22,80,443/tcp && sudo ufw enable)
LOW: Add general *.md deny rule to nginx with explicit exception for known safe files
LOW: Remove dead escapeHtml() function from insert-pages.js and metadata.js
LOW: Remove unused CGI location block from nginx config
LOW: snip.js line 865 — replace innerHTML template literal with textContent/DOM API for defense-in-depth
LOW: Add file size limit check in image-manager.js replaceImage()
LOW: undo-redo.js — enforce MEMORY_BUDGET_BYTES by estimating command payload sizes and evicting oldest when exceeded
LOW: bookmark-editor.js — add recursion depth limit to outlineToTree() and jsonToTree() for defense-in-depth
LOW: bg-borders.js — SVG images accepted by handleImageFile but silently ignored in PDF output. Consider showing a toast warning when SVG is selected, or remove SVG from validTypes.
LOW: clipboard.js — consider adding max paste count limit (e.g., 50 items per page) for defense-in-depth
